sections.heading: Complications
sections.content: {"disease_related": [{"complication": "Bone disease (osteolytic lesions, fractures, cord compression)", "management": "All patients receiving primary myeloma therapy should be given bone-targeting treatment (bisphosphonates [category 1] or denosumab). Continue for up to 2 years. Baseline dental exam recommended. Monitor for ONJ and renal dysfunction. Orthopedic consultation for impending/actual long-bone fractures or cord compression. Palliative RT for uncontrolled pain, impending pathologic fracture, or impending cord compression using 8 Gy x 1 fraction or 20-30 Gy in 5-15 fractions [MYEL-I, MYEL-D]"}, {"complication": "Hypercalcemia", "management": "Treat with hydration, bisphosphonates (zoledronic acid preferred), denosumab, steroids, and/or calcitonin [MYEL-I]"}, {"complication": "Hyperviscosity", "management": "Plasmapheresis as adjunctive therapy for symptomatic hyperviscosity [MYEL-I]"}, {"complication": "Anemia", "management": "Consider erythropoietin for anemic patients [MYEL-I]"}, {"complication": "Renal dysfunction", "management": "Pulse dexamethasone, regimens containing bortezomib and/or daratumumab. Hydration to goal urine output 100-150 cc/h. Discontinue nephrotoxic medications. Treat hypercalcemia and hyperuricemia. Dialysis if refractory. Lenalidomide dose adjustments per renal function: CrCl ≥30 to <60 mL/min: 10 mg daily; CrCl <30 mL/min not on dialysis: 15 mg every 48h; ESRD on dialysis: 5 mg once daily [MYEL-L]"}, {"complication": "CNS disease", "management": "Multimodality therapy (radiation and systemic therapy) with incorporation of palliative care. RT for focal neurologic deficits. WBRT ≤23.4 Gy in 13 fractions for poorly localized disease. IT chemotherapy (Thiotepa/Hydrocortisone or Methotrexate/Cytarabine/Hydrocortisone). IMiDs with CNS penetration (pomalidomide, lenalidomide). CAR-T and BsAb therapy not precluded by treated CNS disease history [MYEL-H, CNSM-1]"}, {"complication": "Venous thromboembolism (VTE)", "management": "VTE prophylaxis for all patients. Risk stratification by IMPEDE or SAVED score. Low risk: aspirin 81-325 mg daily. High risk: LMWH, rivaroxaban 10 mg daily, apixaban 2.5 mg BID, or warfarin (INR 2-3). Duration: indefinite while on myeloma therapy [MYEL-K]"}], "treatment_related": {"chemotherapy": ["Peripheral neuropathy (bortezomib-related, especially twice-weekly dosing; subcutaneous administration preferred to reduce risk)", "Neutropenia and thrombocytopenia (common with lenalidomide, pomalidomide, alkylating agents)", "Increased risk of secondary malignancies especially with lenalidomide maintenance post-HCT"], "immunotherapy": ["Cytokine release syndrome (CRS) with CAR-T cell and bispecific antibody therapies", "Immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome (ICANS) with CAR-T and BsAb", "Higher risk for CRS in Black/African American individuals receiving CAR-T therapy", "Infection risk during and after CAR-T and BsAb therapies"], "targeted_therapy": ["Daratumumab and isatuximab-irfc may interfere with serologic testing causing false-positive indirect Coombs test", "Monoclonal antibodies may produce false-positive serum immunofixation if monoclonal protein is IgG kappa", "Carfilzomib can potentially cause cardiac, renal, and pulmonary toxicities, especially in older patients", "Belantamab mafodotin-blmf: ocular events (keratopathy) notably more common than with daratumumab-based regimens", "Selinexor: thrombocytopenia, fatigue, nausea, anemia"], "surgery": ["Consider surgery for structurally unstable plasmacytomas or neurologic compromise due to mass effect", "Vertebroplasty or kyphoplasty for symptomatic vertebral compression fractures", "Renal biopsy if albuminuria or abnormal renal function when other causes unclear", "Sural nerve biopsy may be considered in certain circumstances for MGNS but associated with pain and potential permanent deficits"], "radiation": ["RT primarily used for palliation in MM", "Careful planning to minimize toxicity to spinal cord, brain, bone marrow, and adjacent OARs", "Systemic therapy should not be delayed for RT; can be used concurrently", "Moderately fractionated courses of 20-25 Gy in 8-10 fractions preferred over higher doses to limit toxicity", "Bridging RT may be considered prior to CAR-T infusion for symptomatic/at-risk disease"]}}

labels: Bone disease (osteolytic lesions, fractures, cord compression)
Peripheral neuropathy (bortezomib-related, especially twice-weekly dosing; subcutaneous administration preferred to reduce risk)
Neutropenia and thrombocytopenia (common with lenalidomide, pomalidomide, alkylating agents)
Increased risk of secondary malignancies especially with lenalidomide maintenance post-HCT
Cytokine release syndrome (CRS) with CAR-T cell and bispecific antibody therapies
Immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome (ICANS) with CAR-T and BsAb
Higher risk for CRS in Black/African American individuals receiving CAR-T therapy
Infection risk during and after CAR-T and BsAb therapies
Daratumumab and isatuximab-irfc may interfere with serologic testing causing false-positive indirect Coombs test
Monoclonal antibodies may produce false-positive serum immunofixation if monoclonal protein is IgG kappa
Carfilzomib can potentially cause cardiac, renal, and pulmonary toxicities, especially in older patients
Belantamab mafodotin-blmf: ocular events (keratopathy) notably more common than with daratumumab-based regimens

cards.name: Bone disease (osteolytic lesions, fractures, cord compression)
cards.detail: All patients receiving primary myeloma therapy should be given bone-targeting treatment (bisphosphonates [category 1] or denosumab). Continue for up to 2 years. Baseline dental exam recommended. Monitor for ONJ and renal dysfunction. Orthopedic consultation for impending/actual long-bone fractures or cord compression. Palliative RT for uncontrolled pain, impending pathologic fracture, or impending cord compression using 8 Gy x 1 fraction or 20-30 Gy in 5-15 fractions [MYEL-I, MYEL-D]

cards.name: Hypercalcemia
cards.detail: Treat with hydration, bisphosphonates (zoledronic acid preferred), denosumab, steroids, and/or calcitonin [MYEL-I]

cards.name: Hyperviscosity
cards.detail: Plasmapheresis as adjunctive therapy for symptomatic hyperviscosity [MYEL-I]

cards.name: Renal dysfunction
cards.detail: Pulse dexamethasone, regimens containing bortezomib and/or daratumumab. Hydration to goal urine output 100-150 cc/h. Discontinue nephrotoxic medications. Treat hypercalcemia and hyperuricemia. Dialysis if refractory. Lenalidomide dose adjustments per renal function: CrCl ≥30 to <60 mL/min: 10 mg daily; CrCl <30 mL/min not on dialysis: 15 mg every 48h; ESRD on dialysis: 5 mg once daily [MYEL-L]

cards.name: CNS disease
cards.detail: Multimodality therapy (radiation and systemic therapy) with incorporation of palliative care. RT for focal neurologic deficits. WBRT ≤23.4 Gy in 13 fractions for poorly localized disease. IT chemotherapy (Thiotepa/Hydrocortisone or Methotrexate/Cytarabine/Hydrocortisone). IMiDs with CNS penetration (pomalidomide, lenalidomide). CAR-T and BsAb therapy not precluded by treated CNS disease history [MYEL-H, CNSM-1]

cards.name: Venous thromboembolism (VTE)
cards.detail: VTE prophylaxis for all patients. Risk stratification by IMPEDE or SAVED score. Low risk: aspirin 81-325 mg daily. High risk: LMWH, rivaroxaban 10 mg daily, apixaban 2.5 mg BID, or warfarin (INR 2-3). Duration: indefinite while on myeloma therapy [MYEL-K]
